title: Accessibility in the AI Ecosystem
description: This session will showcase how AI enhances accessibility for customers, partners, and the disability community, with real-world examples like Microsoft's partnership with Rijksmuseum and the importance of inclusive data sets.

title: Creating Accessible Materials for Students Blind or Low Vision
description: Looking for alternatives towards making worksheets accessible, learn some techniques using digital content creations tools. This presentation incorporates Universal Design principals to accommodate students with an emphasis on visual impairments.

title: From Innovation to Impact: Tools for AT Market Success

presenters.name: Jonathan Pearlman
presenters.affiliation: IMPACT Center at the University of Pittsburgh

presenters.name: Mary Goldberg
presenters.affiliation: IMPACT Center at the University of Pittsburgh

description: Discover practical tools and strategies for navigating the complex process of bringing technologies to market. Attendees will learn to overcome common barriers in tech transfer and apply interdisciplinary collaboration and tools to enhance innovation.

title: If You Build It, They Will Come: Creating an ACR/VPAT Repository
description: Join us as we showcase our new repository for storing, sharing and analyzing ACRs (i.e., VPATs)! We'll explain the process and benefits of operating a centralized ACR repository, and equip you with the knowledge and motivation to create your own.

title: Indoor Navigation System as a Cognitive Mapping Aid for the Blind
description: Most indoor navigation systems primarily focus on providing turn-by-turn guidance. In this work, we reimagine these systems as educational tools to help blind and visually impaired users build cognitive maps of unfamiliar indoor environments.

title: Interpreting Accessibility Metadata for Inclusive Learning
description: Accessibility metadata is a priority for publishers, enabling educators to determine if a book accommodates all students. This data provides insights into accessibility features and conformance, empowering informed decisions for inclusive learning.

title: NVDA 2025: New and Upcoming Features
description: NVDA, the free open-source screen reader for windows keeps growing! Learn about new and future features, major updates to our add-on system and experience, training and support options, community resources and how NV Access maximizes impact and sustainability.

title: The Accessibility Guidelines Landscape: 508, ADA, and WCAG
description: What's the difference between ADA, 508, and WCAG? Learn the basics of U.S. accessibility regulations to get started on your accessibility journey, ask the right questions, evaluate basic requirements, and where to look for continued learning.

title: Unlocking Accessible PDF Org Charts, Flow Charts and Calendars
description: Learn how to create accessible org charts, flow charts, and calendars that exemplify true usability. This session covers best practices for structure, alternative text, color contrast, and keyboard navigation, with real-world examples.

title: Use of RTDs in Educational Assessments for the Blind
description: Learn how Refreshable Tactile Displays (RTDs) may be used in inclusive educational evaluation contexts for future test takers. This new potentially paradigm-shifting technology can work synergistically to make test experiences more equitable for students with visual impairments.

title: User Centered Testing with User Journeys and JAWS Inspect
description: Accessibility testing is often focused on compliance with standards rather than user experience. This session examines how to develop and harness user journeys and assistive technology testing to connect more directly with end users.

title: Verbose AI: The Accessibility Challenge
description: Explore how verbose AI-generated image descriptions could hinder screen reader accessibility. We'll share real-world examples, the importance of context, AI's limitations, and practical solutions to promote inclusivity and encourage action.

title: What's New with Amazon Devices
description: Amazon obsesses about customer delight, and that includes customers with disabilities. In this session, Amazon accessibility experts will discuss the latest accessibility features for Fire TV, Echo and Alexa, and more. We will discuss the latest and review the suite of award-winning accessibility features that have been delighting Amazon's customers with disabilities for years.

title: Accessibility at Prime Video
description: Join the Amazon/Prime Video Accessibility team to hear more about our journey to introduce new features and improve existing accessibility options. Learn about our initiatives to improve the discovery and watch experience for customers.

title: Accessibility Partnership: More than Just Compliance
description: Partnering with accessibility experts helped drive organization-wide commitment to accessibility at Amplify. Learn how building an accessibility program with internal and external experts support your accessibility goals, changing compliance standards.

title: AI and AT: Balancing Security with Productivity
description: Join this interactive session where companies share experiences on navigating AI adoption in assistive technology. Explore how to balance security, privacy, and productivity while fostering collaboration on best practices for people who are blind.

title: Avoiding the Pitfalls of "Shift-left" Accessibility
description: If you've included accessibility from the start but struggle to maintain it, this session is for you. We'll share Microsoft's strategies and introduce "Continuous Accessibility" to keep accessibility practices throughout the product lifecycle.

title: Creating a Culture of Digital Accessibility

presenters.name: Jacy Meador
presenters.affiliation: Texas Parks & Wildlife Department - State Parks Division

presenters.name: Cynthia Pickens
presenters.affiliation: Texas Parks & Wildlife Department - State Parks Division

description: Hear about the Texas State Parks digital media team's long journey towards digital accessibility, from laying the groundwork to integration of digital accessibility into all aspects of the team's work.

title: Cross-Sector Strategies for Accessibility Skills Education
description: This panel brings together experts from diverse sectors to talk about how they're closing the accessibility skills gap. Attendees will learn practical solutions for improving accessibility education within their organizations and industries.

title: Eye-Tracking eSports for Gamers with Severe Disabilities
description: We have developed a system that enables individuals with severe physical limitations to operate PCs and gaming consoles using eye-tracking for aiming and switches operated with minimal force. This session presents an overview of this innovative system.

title: Getting Started with Keyboard Accessibility
description: This session is an introduction to keyboard accessibility, and will present a methodology for testing the WCAG requirements for keyboard accessibility. It will include tools that can aid in diagnosing keyboard accessibility issues.

title: OneCourt: Real-Time Access to Live Sports
description: OneCourt makes live sports accessible to blind and low vision fans by turning game data into vibrations and audio. At home or in the stadium, fans can feel the action with their fingertips. Join us in shaping the future of accessible entertainment!

title: Open-Source Tool for Free Computer Accessibility Tools
description: Learn about the amazing range of computer/mobile accessibility tools available for all computers and mobile devices and the free tool for discovering which fit the needs of individuals or schools based on needs, computers/mobile devices, and cost.

title: Accessible Haptic-Spatial UI Paradigms for ICTs
description: In this session, we will explore the pain points of blind and low vision users when navigating graphical user interfaces. We will learn about design solutions that provide spacial properties to improve accessibility and a model based on our findings.

title: AI and AD (Audio Description): For Good or Ill?

presenters.name: Joel Snyder
presenters.affiliation: Audio Description Associates, LLC / American Council of the Blind's Audio Description Project

description: How can AI be used (for good or ill?) for audio description (AD) in media? Are there special considerations that come into play when using speech synthesis? What about using AI to craft the AD language itself?

title: Analysis of 2D and 3D Datasets for Sign Language Recognition
description: In this study, we collected both 2D RGB and 3D stereoscopic depth data from 15 ASL signer participants. Overall, while using 3D data was slightly more effective, it was not worth the time, effort, and computational costs of using 3D data over 2D.

title: Are Design Systems the Key to Sustained Web Accessibility?
description: Does having an accessible design system translate to having accessible digital products? Or is it that organizations which can afford a design system simply have more resources to invest in web accessibility? Let's explore some of the evidence.

title: Creating Barrier Free PDFs from Microsoft Word
description: Leverage the features of Microsoft Word to harness the accessibility features of the program and to help you ensure that PDF files created from that Word document are as accessible as possible. Always start with the source!

title: Empowering Your CIO to Meet New Demands Imposed by Title II
description: Join us as we explore your CIO's directive to craft a university policy ensuring compliance with Title II of the ADA. Learn how to identify accessible publications, meet legal standards, and foster an inclusive academic environment for all students.

title: Enhancing Communication in Aphasia: Embracing Multiple Modalities
description: This presentation explores the effectiveness of integrating AAC into multimodal communication for individuals with aphasia. The presentation will focus on supportive conversation techniques and creating customized communication plans.

title: Growing Your Career in Accessibility Beyond Testing
description: Join our Amazon panelists if you're curious about your career choices beyond QA testing as they discuss various impactful roles and opportunities to help advance accessibility in tech.

title: How We Represent Users with Disabilities: User Personas
description: Using real examples, you will learn how to create accessibility personas. Learn the value they bring, how to involve persons with disabilities in user research, and how to tailor personas to your domains and distribute them across an organization.

title: Testing for Digital Accessibility
description: Explore practical strategies for testing digital accessibility. Learn to identify and report common accessibility issues using manual and automated test methods, to ensure compatibility with assistive technologies across platforms.

title: Toward Disability Inclusive Generative AI Systems
description: Provides an overview of how to make generative AI more disability inclusive, including measuring how AI represents people with disabilities and exploring how accessibility can be integrated into the design process for creating AI systems.

title: User Study: Smart Speakers and Visual Disabilities
description: This session explores usage patterns, challenges, and opportunities related to smart speakers for individuals with visual disabilities.

title: Web Accessibility Ratings by Industry
description: TPGi shares web accessibility benchmarking for private-sector industries, U.S. government (.gov), and education (.edu).

title: Featured Presentation: What's New in Google Accessibility
description: Join us to hear announcements from several Google teams about products, tools and features. We've made a lot of progress in the last year and we're excited to share a few highlights with you all!

title: 40 Years of Adaptive Tech, What Does the Future Hold?
description: History of tech for the visually impaired from braillers to smart phones and beyond.

title: AI and Future of Work for Persons with Disabilities
description: The evolution of AI into business processes is introducing a new way to work that can potentially remove barriers that have long existed for persons with disabilities. See how AI can be implemented to simplify complex navigation and hunting for information.

title: Automating WCAG Remediation: What's Actually Possible with AI?
description: This session will explore what AI can achieve in automated WCAG remediation, where human expertise is still needed, and how a hybrid approach can accelerate compliance for developers, businesses, and accessibility professionals.

title: BARD Mobile: Carry a Library in Your Pocket

presenters.name: Donald Olson
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

presenters.name: Elizabeth Bottner
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

description: This session shows how BARD Mobile offers NLS patrons the freedom to explore the national collection anywhere, at any time. We will demonstrate how to find books, and then how to customize the reading experience so it's just right.

title: Design AT: Increase Attitudes for People with Disabilities
description: Design thinking begins with understanding the user's experience of the problem. This session will show how students inventing AT develop an understanding of challenges that other individuals have and create positive attitudes in the process.

title: Digital Accessibility Trends and Insights for 2025
description: Digital accessibility is no longer a niche consideration—it’s a global movement reshaping industries, cultures, and expectations. In this session, we’ll explore the latest global trends, from emerging AI advancements to the groundbreaking European Accessibility Act. We’ll also highlight examples, statistics and data related to cultural, industry and tech trends in the field of accessibility. We'll end focusing on the future of accessibility, talking about what’s changed in 2024, what to expect coming into 2025, and how you can continue that momentum. Join us as we celebrate the milestones of our growing community.

title: Gait with a Walker under Cognitive Load and Physical Fatigue
description: The objective of this session is to present an analysis of gait velocity and stride time variability for the walker-assisted gait in cognitively demanding and physically fatiguing conditions to engineers and physical therapists.

title: Get More Out of Reading with Kindle's Accessibility Features
description: What's new with Kindle accessibility? Learn how recent innovations and updates are creating a more accessible experience for Kindle readers. And discover (or re-discover) your favorite Kindle accessibility features that create more opportunities for customers with disabilities.

title: Inclusive Talents: Methodology for School Education on Disability

presenters.name: Javier Pereira
presenters.affiliation: CITIC. Talionis Research Group. Universidade da Coruña

description: We present our experience from a collaborative project between students and people with disabilities aimed at creating low-cost assistive technologies and accessible content. The results will be presented with more than 30 replicable examples.

title: Just-in-Time Faculty Support for Creating Accessible Content
description: Faculty are responsible for creating accessible content, but often lack the skill. Learn how you can provide on-demand support for creating accessible learning experiences directly in the learning management system.

title: Kiosk Accessibility: What Drives It and What Are the Solutions
description: This presentation explores the key drivers behind kiosk accessibility, including regulatory frameworks, user needs, and technological advancements.

title: Scaling Accessibility: Expanding Your Stakeholder Map
description: Engaging diverse stakeholders in accessibility programs is essential for scaling and sustainability. Join us to explore how collaboration and inclusivity can enhance your initiatives and drive positive business outcomes for a more accessible future.

title: The Diverse Tech and Disability Productivity Collision
description: This session explores the gap between tech design and productive usability for disabled leaders. It highlights the need for inclusive training on advanced digital tools and proposes strategies to bridge the disconnect between designers, developers, and assistive technology trainers for improved productivity.

title: Vistas Along the Way: A Journey in Digital Accessibility
description: Are you wondering what a career in digital accessibility could look like? Is your team starting an accessibility program? Have you faced domain-specific issues that don't have popular accessible solutions? Join me as I recap my travels through accessibility.

title: Voices Unlocked: Empowering Neurodivergent Individuals with VR/XR
description: Join "Voices Unlocked" to explore how VR/XR empowers neurodivergent individuals, including those with autism and ADHD. Discover real-world successes, learn best practices, and find inspiration to promote inclusion through innovative technology.

title: Accessible Flowcharts: A Feasibility Study with BVI Participants
description: Learn how accessible flowcharts improve comprehension for BVI individuals. This session covers user-tested methods like textual summaries, Q&A interfaces, navigable diagrams, and tactile graphics, offering actionable insights for educators and designers.

title: Aspirational AT: Dreaming About the Future of Screen Readers
description: Let's explore the future of assistive technology with AI-driven navigation, where innovation meets accessibility for enhanced experiences!

title: Building Accessible Personal Websites and Social Media Presence
description: Designed for anyone interested in digital accessibility. Provides practical tips on making personal websites and social media more accessible, from immediate actions to strategic planning.

title: Declawing MathCAT: A Gentle Intro to Math in NVDA and JAWS
description: Learn how to listen to, interact with, and braille math in JAWS and NVDA. Discover options to customize speech, braille, and navigation to your needs. Hear about future directions for math support. Bring your questions and suggestions!

title: Digital Accessibility Law: A Legal Update and Hot Topics
description: The accessibility standards landscape can be challenging to navigate. We'll look back over the last 25 years of accessibility standards, and discuss how best to make use of today's (and tomorrow's) standards.

title: Evidence-Based Adaptive Transcription for Sign Language Users
description: This research presents an automatic assistive speech-to-text technology tailored for sign language users, enhancing communication by adapting text to match their specific vocabulary and grammar needs, improving accessibility in real-time.

title: Implementing WCAG Tests: From ACT Rules Specifications to Code
description: What are the difficulties in building tools and methodologies for accessibility testing? Can we solve them through ACT rules, a middle layer between WCAG and the actual checks, aiming at clarification of the technical aspects inherent to each media?

title: Integrating AI to Improve Efficiency in Special Education
description: In this session, learn how AI is utilized to reduce the time spent on special education paperwork and streamline special education processes. See firsthand how AI is being used to improve the efficiency of the Individualized Education Program (IEP).

title: Practicing Accessibility Research with Confidence
description: It can feel daunting to apply existing UX principles and methodologies to accessibility research with assistive technology users. This session offers a reflective framework for building meaningful inclusion within your accessibility practice.

title: Reaching Every Learner Through Accessible and Equitable AI
description: AI makes learning more accessible in exciting ways through modal translation, personalization and natural language processing. AI also brings in risks when the AI tools and systems used in education are not built accessibly and equitably. Let's chat!

title: Risks of Isolation and Loneliness for People with Hearing Loss
description: This session will discuss the U.S. Surgeon General's 2023 Advisory around the effects of loneliness and isolation and will explore the potential impact hearing loss can have when coupled with other chronic conditions.

title: Steam Ahead: Conducting Trainings for Access and Inclusion
description: Disabled people have stories to tell that build empathy and set your training on the right track. Learn the questions to cover in accessibility and inclusion training. Participate in a mini storytelling workshop to create meaningful content.

title: The Future of Axe Tools
description: After more than 23 years of experience with our tools, training, and expertise for digital accessibility, Deque has reached a significant acceleration point. Axe accessibility testing tools, including axe-core, the world's most popular accessibility testing library.

title: Web A11y Framework: Understand, Organize, Improve & Communicate
description: Discover how The Web Accessibility Framework can help your organization achieve and maintain its web accessibility goals. Learn practical strategies to define goals, embed processes, and conduct regular testing to ensure your websites are accessible.

title: What's New with Alexa?
description: Alexa is for everyone. Come and learn about how Alexa can serve as an effective personal assistant for people with disabilities.

title: Win with Metrics: How to Track and Show the Value of Your Program
description: Learn how to tell a story with data and which metrics will make a compelling case to your leadership. Understanding how to track and present data are essential to elevating your accessibility program within your organization.

title: A Guide to Simplifying LMS Document Accessibility
description: Discover how DocMD LMS simplifies document accessibility. Learn about automated testing, seamless remediation, and compliance. Create inclusive learning experiences for learning management systems.

title: A Practical Approach to EAA Compliance
description: Discuss the current state of the European Accessibility Act (EAA) and what companies can be doing to ensure they meet the deadline.

title: Accelerating Accessibility Conformance with Open-Source Tools
description: This session covers Equalify's approach to faster, cost-effective web accessibility testing using raw code analysis and autoscaling systems. Attendees will learn practical strategies for implementing open-source tools to improve web accessibility.

title: Accessibility in RFPs and SOWs: A Must-Have, Not a Nice-to-Have
description: Learn how to bake accessibility into your technology projects from the start! This session provides practical guidance on embedding accessibility requirements in RFPs and SOWs, ensuring inclusive and user-friendly technology for everyone.

title: Actually Shifting Left: Technical Accessibility for Designers
description: Designers of web things, working in software like Figma, haven't yet been presented the right information to achieve more technically accessible outcomes in their work. This talk has the lofty (hopeful!) aim of righting that wrong.

title: AI for Inclusion: Strategies and Tools
description: AI is evolving at light speed and is hard to keep up with the changes. Learn about strategies to harness the power of AI tools to promote inclusion for people with disabilities.

title: Amazon Accessibility and Marketing: The Importance of Mechanisms
description: Explore practical marketing mechanisms, resource needs, and strategic checkpoints to proactively address accessibility. Walk away with a plan to make your marketing shine, no matter your industry or role.

title: Co-designing Process of Screen Reader-Friendly Apps

presenters.name: Takahiro Miura
presenters.affiliation: National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST)

description: This session presents the effectiveness of a co-design approach involving visually impaired developers and users in creating high-usability applications.

title: Creating an Accessible Storytelling Platform
description: Join with us for fun session where we will show Feeling Through Studio platform. Feeling Through Studio is an accessible learning platform with multiple options for diverse users who are blind and DeafBlind.

title: Implementing a Document Accessibility Program
description: This session outlines a comprehensive strategy for addressing document accessibility through True Up, Catch Up, and Keep Up phases.

title: Innovating Assistive Tech: AI Data and Connected Solutions
description: This session will explore AI, data metrics, and ecosystem connectivity in assistive technology. Attendees will gain insights into current trends, share their needs, and help shape the future of tools for individuals with visual impairments.

title: Let's Code with LEGO Braille Bricks! New Activities
description: Through interactive brick-based activities, you will discover how LEGO Braille Bricks can be used to develop amazing coding skills amongst students. You will explore the Power of Play and Coding using bricks

title: WCAG Unwrapped: A People's Guide to Accessible Digital Experiences
description: This interactive training provides participants with a fundamental understanding of WCAG through a person-first approach. By exploring real-life scenarios, participants will learn how to apply WCAG principles to create accessible digital experiences.

title: What AI Has to Say About Video Magnification Trends
description: We asked Google Gemini, "What are the current trends for video magnification for people with vision loss?". It provided a remarkably accurate list of trends. We will discuss specific devices and case study applications that reflect these trends.

title: What's New and Coming Up in JAWS ZoomText and Fusion
description: Live demonstration along with question and answer time for the latest and greatest features of JAWS, ZoomText, and Fusion. We will also look ahead to what is coming next.

title: Copilot and AI Tools to Empower Users with Disabilities
description: Join us to discover how Microsoft 365 Copilot empowers the disability community by enhancing accessibility and productivity. Learn about real-world applications, user testimonials, and future developments. Experience live demonstrations insight.

title: Decoding Commonly Confused Terms in WCAG
description: This session clarifies commonly confused WCAG terms like "label" vs. "name," "content change" vs. "context change," and "bounding box" vs. "perimeter." Come learn why understanding these terms (and others) is an essential part of understanding WCAG.

title: Designing Inclusive Games: RNIB's Accessible Gaming Devkit
description: Discover how RNIB's Accessible Gaming Devkit can help you design inclusive games for players with sight loss. This session covers practical strategies, case studies, and real-world applications that ensure accessibility is embedded from the start.

title: Driving Innovation for an Inclusive Tomorrow
description: Sony will present worldwide comprehensive activities in the area of Accessibility which includes:Inclusive Design, Products and Services, and Collaboration with Advocacy groups.

title: Earning Trust with Effective Prioritization Frameworks
description: Data driven prioritization frameworks earn trust with leaders. Come learn about prioritization frameworks, why they work, how they must evolve, and how to effectively leverage them as a key element of your accessibility program.

title: Empowering Abilities Through STEM

presenters.name: John Karr
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the Blind

presenters.name: Katrina Best
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the Blind

presenters.name: Jason Martin
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the Blind

description: Explore how to create inclusive STEM opportunities for students with visual impairments. This session covers accessible coding stages, best teaching practices, and essential resources to support student success in diverse learning environments.

title: Exploring Accessible Explainable AI: Promising Avenues
description: We will explore the concept of Accessible Explainable Artificial Intelligence (AXAI), focusing on audio description and audio XAI approaches. The aim is to discuss how these technologies can aid individuals with sight loss and enhance inclusion.

title: Exploring Reasonable Accommodations Through Accessible Esports

presenters.name: Takahiro Miura
presenters.affiliation: National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST)

description: The presenters talk about a case study of a course designed to promote understanding of reasonable accommodation through esports (fighting action game) with visual impairment accessibility.

title: Harnessing Artificial Intelligence for Greater Accessibility
description: Explore AI's role in accessibility through image recognition and captioning. Learn to identify biases in AI systems and understand their real-world impact. Gain insights on implementing inclusive AI solutions across various fields.

title: New and Innovative Low Vision Solutions from Eschenbach
description: This session will provide an overview of some of the newer and innovative low vision solutions from Eschenbach including Optaro, a new electronic magnifier that connects to an iPhone and will end with a hands-on session with select devices.

title: Procurement - The Neglected Piece of the Accessibility Puzzle
description: Procurement is a key dimension of accessibility maturity. If you're a procurer, or just as a user, of ICT purchased for your organization, come and learn how to improve the accessibility quality of future purchases.

title: Regional Collaboration Is Crucial for Effective ICT Support
description: Collaborative support is crucial for efficient ICT support for persons with disabilities in a local community with a few AT specialists. This session provides such a scheme based on intermediate outreach and AT seminars for teachers and co-medicals.

title: The Barriers of Babel: How Language Intersects Accessibility
description: When you have a disability, the language you speak or sign affects your access drastically. Investigating 100 languages broadly and 10 in depth, this session offers technical solutions for a range of population sizes, writing systems, and regions.

title: The ROI of Accessibility
description: By making your website accessible, your organization can capture a huge, overlooked market share, reduce your legal risk, lower call-center costs, and boost brand value.

title: Understanding Burnout in Accessibility Work
description: In response to the rise in burnout in accessibility specialists, we have conducted a study exploring patterns, causes, and mitigation strategies for burnout. During this session, we will present our findings and unravel the reality of burnout in this field.

title: WCAG 3: The True Story!
description: Work continues on the W3C Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 3. The Accessibility Guidelines Working Group Chairs and Team Contact will give you the low down on the pit falls and progress on the latest update to the internationally recognized WCAG 3.

title: 50 Years of Accessibility Innovation in Public Television
description: Media accessibility has changed significantly over the past 50 years, as has the viewer's responses to it. This session will explore this history and future, and the intersection of public media, children's television, and disability representation.

title: A Call to Action: The Case for Accessibility Bounties of FOSS Dev
description: This talk will provide an overview of the current state of accessibility in Free Open Source Software (FOSS), highlighting the barriers and misaligned incentives that prevent meaningful contributions by accessibility experts.

title: Closing the Digital Accessibility Gap: College to Work
description: This session explores the gap between IT education and workforce demands for digital accessibility expertise. It makes the case for embedding accessibility into IT curricula, based on lessons learned from internships and real-world experiences.

title: Designing Inclusive Experiences from a Neurodivergent Designer
description: Gain insights from a neurodivergent designer on creating inclusive digital experiences. Learn practical strategies to design for diverse cognitive needs, enhancing usability and empathy in your design process.

title: Enhancing Education Through Accessibyte’s Comprehensive Platform
description: Learn how Accessibyte's online platform gamifies typing skills (Typio, Braillio, Accessibyte Arcade) and enhances study materials (Quick Cards, ProPack) for students with a broad range of needs, including those who are blind or visually impaired.

title: Expanding DEI+A with Age and Belonging
description: Enhance your DEI+A program by fostering belonging, addressing intersectionality, and including employees over 40. Learn how to promote inclusive leadership, challenge ageism, and implement effective initiatives to create a truly inclusive workplace.

title: Packaging Accessibility: Sharing Amazon's Learnings
description: In this session we share Amazon's learnings on packaging accessibility. We will explore results from our cross-disciplinary research with customers with disabilities, and principles we developed to make unboxing a delightful experience.

title: Personalization of Accessible Charts and Graphs
description: Often in dataviz designs, what is more accessible to one person can be worse for another. What if you didn't have to compromise? We share usability research into personalizable charts on the web, a modern solution to this problem.

title: Reviewing VPATs; Vendor Selection and Compliance for Procurement
description: This session will help procurement professionals navigate the complexities of evaluating third-party vendors for accessibility compliance.

title: Single-User Centered Design of a Motivational Assistant

presenters.name: Dominique Archambault
presenters.affiliation: Laboratoire CHArt, Université Paris 8 Vincennes Saint-Denis

description: A prototype software designed for a specific user with visual impairment and autism will be presented, enabling him to access enjoyable and motivational entertainment on a standard smartphone.

title: Systematically Evaluating Digital Map Tools Based on WCAG

presenters.name: Brandon Biggs
presenters.affiliation: Smith-Kettlewell Eye Research Institute and Georgia Institute of Technology

description: This presentation gives digital map customers a toolkit to articulate accessibility needs, and shows digital map providers how to make their maps accessible.

title: The State of Mobile App Accessibility in 2025
description: Discover the most common accessibility issues in top mobile apps across industries. Learn how these barriers affect users with disabilities and gain actionable strategies to improve app accessibility within your organization from app experts.

title: Using AI in Mobility Trainings
description: Discover how WeWALK's AI Mobility (AIM) technology enhances mobility training for visually impaired individuals. This session will cover AIM's real-time data tracking, enabling Orientation and Mobility (O&M) Specialists to remotely monitor progress.

title: Where to Find Accessible Educational Materials for Students
description: Sourcing Accessible Educational Materials (AEM) can be challenging. Learn best practices for finding and using AEM to support diverse student needs.

title: Balancing Ethics and Risk in Assistive Tech with AI
description: How do you manage products with an imperfect technology like AI, where things can go seriously wrong?This session will provide a real-world framework for understanding and managing AI's impact on people with disabilities.

title: Build Inclusive Talent Via Community Testing in an AI World

presenters.name: Suyash Ekre
presenters.affiliation: LightHouse for the Blind and Visually Impaired (San Francisco)

presenters.name: Sean Dougherty
presenters.affiliation: LightHouse for the Blind and Visually Impaired (San Francisco)

presenters.name: Corvin Bazgan
presenters.affiliation: LightHouse for the Blind and Visually Impaired (San Francisco)

description: Discover how LightHouse SF is reimagining accessibility testing by integrating the BLV community's expertise. Explore our innovative model that combines technical knowledge with lived experiences of the community to enhance usability for all.

title: Building Empathy with Stakeholders to Drive Accessibility
description: How can we help leaders really "get" the need for digital accessibility? In this highly interactive session, we'll learn tools to build support for your a11y program and practice techniques to align a11y with your organizations culture and affect real change.

title: Building More Accessible Products with AWS AI
description: Take a deep dive into a suite of AI/ML services provided by Amazon Web Service (AWS). We will take a closer look at these services and feature two demonstrations of how AWS customers can add accessibility features to their own products.

title: Embedding Accessibility into the Next Generation of BBC Apps
description: Learn how the BBC, the world's largest public sector broadcaster, approaches embedding accessibility at scale on six iOS and Android apps, how native components can fall short and how our work on products informs our accessibility guidelines.

title: Engaging Gamers with Visual Impairments in Console Gaming

presenters.name: Drew Metzger
presenters.affiliation: Augusta VA Medical Center - Blind Rehab Virtual Assistive Technology Hub

description: Attendees will learn about visual impairments and how to teach persons with visual impairments how to successfully play console video games. Strategies on how to play will be demonstrated, and successful case studies will be discussed.

title: Glean: Using AI Responsibly to Help Students Make Better Notes
description: Glean's AI-powered note-taking tool empowers students to overcome information overload by effortlessly capturing content and creating personalized study resources. Glean leverages AI responsibly to enhance, not replace, student learning.

title: Invisible Accessibility: The Parts They Don't See
description: Person "X" has been leading the accessibility efforts at company "Y" for 6 months, how come their website still isn't accessible? Rolling out a sustainable Accessibility Program takes time. Whether it's creating awareness, upskilling teams, buy-in from stakeholders, or building resources.

title: MAC-U-Vision+: An Improved Application for Individuals with AMD

presenters.name: Zhigang Zhu
presenters.affiliation: Department of Computer Science, City College of New York, CUNY

description: MAC-U-Vision+ supports patients with Age-related Macular Degeneration (AMD) through the early stages with information and social support, to gamification of oculomotor control training and accessibility tools, among others. Our application development is guided by a user study.

title: New Horizons in Discussing Technology: Re-Imagining and Re-Framing
description: In this interactive presentation you will be empowered to be a thought leader on how we think about, and discuss, technology and access. You will also engage with how different ways of framing these concepts has immense practical significance.

title: NSF Convergence Accelerator Assistive Technology Showcase
description: Come experience new assistive technologies that are being developed through the NSF's Convergence Accelerator Track H: Enhancing Opportunities for Persons with Disabilities. Meet the teams, demo the technologies, and find ways to collaborate!

title: Nurturing a Culture that Collaborates for Inclusive Experiences
description: How do we develop a culture of inclusive collaboration? Through people that build processes, are accountable, seek education, and will strive to focus on the customer. Join to discuss the "how" of nurturing a team and environment that self-organizes and collaborates to deliver accessible experiences naturally.

title: Talk to Me JAWS: Tips to Make Documents Screen Reader Friendly
description: "Talk to Me, JAWS!" offers 10 practical tips to improve document accessibility for screen reader users. Learn simple strategies to enhance navigation, readability, and usability, making your documents more inclusive and screen reader-friendly.

title: The State of Assistive Technology for People with Vision Loss

presenters.name: Sharon Giovinazzo
presenters.affiliation: Lighthouse for the Blind and Visually Impaired San Francisco

description: Explore the current state and trends of assistive technology for people who are blind or visually impaired. Learn how innovation and integration of AI, navigation technologies, wearables and mainstream tech is transforming development.

title: What Do You See in UC Disability Websites?
description: University disability websites can serve as the first contact for students with disabilities. This session shares the criteria and content analysis of research in the system's disability services websites. Discover best practices for these websites.

title: Accessibility Beyond Purchase: Ensuring Products for Everyone
description: You reviewed the Accessible Conformance Reports / VPATs and selected a vendor that is the most accessible. What should you do next? Come and learn what comes next to continue to improve the accessibility of procured products.

title: Accessibility in Higher Ed Purchasing with the New HECVAT
description: Join to learn how accessibility, security, and privacy risk reviews can be aligned to benefit software purchasing in higher education. For vendors, learn how you can streamline providing technical documentation and convey your accessibility efforts.

title: AI Assistance for Improving Digital Accessibility
description: Our work emphasizes sharing process improvements to meet all the accessibility needs that a product may have. Some of the related details have also been published in the Design for All, Institute of India, June 2020, newsletter [5].

title: Bringing Statistics to the Rest of Us
description: Statistics and visualization are ubiquitous in education, science, and other areas, yet major accessibility barriers remain. We will review work on making statistics accessible and demonstrate a new approach to what a born accessible field could be.

title: Crafting Seamless Keyboard Shortcuts for a Global Audience
description: Elevate your web app's accessibility with seamless keyboard shortcuts. Learn key techniques for intuitive design, localization, and conflict prevention, straight from the developers who built shortcuts for a leading global e-commerce platform.

title: Don't Be a Digital Access Karen: Manage Your Brand Reputation
description: Perception goes beyond messaging-it's about making content accessible to all. Learn how digital accessibility can boost inclusivity, drive positive PR, and expand your audience. Leave with the tools to elevate your brand and achieve results.

title: Ensuring Accessibility for STEM Content: Inclusive Design
description: This session explores the challenges of making STEM content accessible and provides practical strategies for designing inclusive course materials, including accessible formats for assessments and exams.

title: Find Your Friends: Grow Your Organization's Accessibility Network

presenters.name: Kelly Melcher
presenters.affiliation: State of Minnesota Department of Human Services

description: The path to build your digital accessibility maturity can be long and lonely. Partners can help expand your reach and get more done. Join me to learn where to look for these friends, get them join you, and how to keep moving the work forward!

title: Haptic Feedback in Movies with a Sony PS5 Controller
description: Closed captioning efficiently conveys spoken dialogue but often leaves out crucial non-speech information (NSI) such as background noises. We evaluate the effectiveness of haptic feedback in conveying NSI using a Sony PlayStation 5 controller.

title: Impacts of the ADA Title II Final Ruling
description: The ADA Title II act means websites and mobile apps provided by public institutions must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards by April 2026. We will explore the ruling and how institutions and vendors can work together to meet the requirements of the ruling.

title: Pioneering Progress: Digital Accessibility Metrics and KPIs
description: Bring your digital accessibility program to the next level by measuring what matters. Learn how to align metrics with milestones, write Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), and effectively communicate progress of digital accessibility efforts.

title: Reading and Writing Math with a Screen Reader: 2nd Edition
description: Join for the sequel and witness JAWS and NVDA tangle with math in Word. After we found gaps at last year's CSUN, Microsoft developers joined the team and dug in. We'll showcase impressive progress that enhances the experience for blind students.

title: Transitioning to PC Skills with a Hybrid Braille Display

presenters.name: Jennifer Wenzel
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the Blind

presenters.name: Sarah Welch
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the Blind

description: Learn about the role of a hybrid braille display as a student transitions to using a PC with a screen reader. Understand how these displays encourage student problem-solving and independent learning as well as reenforcing key computer concepts.

title: Vibration-Based Hazard Warning System for Visually Impaired
description: This session introduces a system that uses vibrations to inform the visually impaired of the direction and distance of obstacles. The presentation covers the system design, shares the results of field tests, and includes a demonstration.

title: When Web Accessibility Enthusiasm Actively Harms
description: We will delve into the challenges faced by ADHD users when interacting with digital technologies, exploring potential solutions.

title: Featured Presentation: Digital Accessibility Legal Update: US
description: Digital access is a civil right of disabled people. This session shares U.S. legal updates about access to websites, apps, kiosks, and more, and about laws, regulations, lawsuits, negotiations, and government policies.

title: A 3D Printer Build Lesson for Students with Visual Impairments
description: Learn about the design and study of a unique lesson where high school students with visual impairments in 12 states build and use 3D printers. We discuss data on the STEM practices students used and remaining accessibility barriers to 3D printing.

title: ASR Captioning and Free Tools: How Leaders Use Existing Technology
description: This session focuses on original research gathered from 200 leaders using ASR and captioning and explores its intersection with delivering accessibility. Discover which ASRs to avoid and which to embrace to craft more inclusive student experiences.

title: Designing Accessible Infographics and Data Visualizations
description: This session will explore the principles and techniques for creating accessible infographics and data visualizations for both print and web. Attendees will leave with actionable strategies and tools to design data that is impactful.

title: EyeMoT: Expanding Potential for Children with Disabilities
description: Supporting the ""I Did It!"" moments for children and individuals with severe disabilities, let's expand the possibilities for people with severe disabilities with this app!

title: From Print to Inclusive: Simplifying Multiformat Conversion
description: Learn how to efficiently create, validate and distribute accessible EPUB, PDF, Braille, and more, reducing production time from months to minutes, with no in-depth technical expertise required.

title: ICT Procurement Maturity: Necessitating Vendor Accountability
description: Discover how to transfer resource-heavy accessibility evaluations to product vendors, minimizing the burden on purchasing institutions. We will outline this process's importance within the Accessibility Maturity Model.

title: Inclusio: Towards Interoperability of Accessible Graphics
description: Creating accessible graphics is difficult. Inclusio, a multi-team project, makes it easy to generate and distribute interoperable, inclusive graphics with our end-to-end, AI-driven marketplace, open metadata format, and multimodal output systems.

title: InftyReader Online Making PDF/PowerPoint Accessible
description: This web site offers an innovative online service to convert inaccessible STEM contents in PDF/PowerPoint (PPT) into various accessible forms such as "Fixed-Layout DAISY," PPT slides with TTS aloud reading, Markdown text with AsciiMath, etc.

title: Interactive Charts for Everyone
description: A pie chart with 4 slices and a stacked line chart with millions of data points require vastly different approaches to accessibility. Highcharts and Elsevier share user research into modern solutions for dataviz accessibility.

title: Redefining Design: Accessibility as a Growth Opportunity
description: Learn what it takes to upskill 500+ designers on accessibility, integrate it into role expectations, and build a sustainable support system. The talk will help you drive the organizational change and shift left accessibility practices.

title: The Princeton Approach to Effective Digital Accessibility
description: Princeton shares its digital accessibility program successes and lessons so that colleagues can adapt its approach, which focuses on focus on values, goals, and partnerships, to their own institutions.

title: The Role of User Experience in the Adoption of AATs
description: We estimate increases in user experience needed to incentivize investments in AATs such as indoor wayfinding systems. Such provisions by enablers have the potential to benefit persons with disabilities which can offset accessibility investments.

title: Transforming Document Remediation: Expert-Led and AI-Driven
description: Explore how AI is revolutionizing document remediation with PREP, which auto-tags 90% of documents for compliance. Join us to see how PREP streamlines workflows, cuts down time and effort and achieves accessibility at scale across diverse industries.

title: What It Really Means to Shift Left: Transforming Your Program
description: Sometimes it feels like we're stuck in an endless audit and remediation cycle and never make true progress. Everyone says that you should 'shift left' - but how do you that in real life? What if you could structure your program to shift left and more?

title: A Mobile App to Facilitate Non-Visual Perception of 2D Shapes
description: We present ShapeInsight2D, a novel mobile application that uses touchscreen gestures, along with auditory and haptic feedback, to help individuals who are blind or visually impaired perceive and understand 2D shapes in digital images.

title: AI for People with Vision Loss: Real-World and Development
description: Explore how AI is revolutionizing technology for people who are blind or visually impaired. This session highlights current AI tech, its real-world use, user experience, research, concerns and future direction.

title: CANCELLED: Accessibility Debt: Developing a Strategic Roadmap
description: This session covers strategies for building a roadmap to address accessibility debt, including gap analyses, audits, SOP refinements, and stakeholder engagement. Attendees will learn to shift from risk management to proactive, sustainable solutions.

title: Chopping Down Accessibility Obstacles with Axe
description: Join us for an interactive product feedback and Q&A session. Your input on Deque's axe will help us "sharpen" our approach and "clear the way" for digital accessibility!

title: Common Mistakes in Web Accessibility
description: We analyzed approximately 30,000 messages from consultants to clients regarding automated web accessibility checks. Our study reveals common mistakes in organizations with accessibility programs, offering insights beyond the WebAIM Million study.

title: Creating Practical Accessibility Materials for Product Designers
description: Shifting accessibility work 'left' involves building designs that are more accessible. But accessible product design requires knowledgeable and well-equipped product designers. So how do we equip designers? Join this session to explore that and more!

title: Design Systems: An Accessibility Team's Best Friend
description: This session will explore how accessibility and design systems teams can collaborate to overcome mutual challenges. Attendees will learn practical strategies to improve collaboration, unblock shared roadblocks, and deliver better products.

title: How the CSU System Is Preparing for April 24, 2026
description: With just over a year to the deadline for the Title II changes to the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), learn how a large public university system, along with one of its largest and smallest campuses, are working to meet these new requirements.

title: How to Create an Accessibility Self-Service Mechanism
description: Product, design, and tech teams are accountable for delivering accessible experiences, in some they lack guidance on how to do it themselves. Accessibility resources in organizations are often limited, and demand for Accessible expertise and services is always increasing.

title: Improving Accessibility of Digital Courseware Through UX Research
description: Measuring product accessibility goes far beyond compliance; it involves engaging with customers with disabilities. Learn how we conduct UX research on digital courseware to improve the experience of students and instructors with disabilities.

title: Integrating Accessibility Management in Enterprise CMS
description: This session will guide organizations through integrating accessibility into CMS workflows, maintaining version control across platforms and languages, and ensuring compliance by testing web content and PDFs.

title: JAWS and ZoomText Enterprise Deployment, Five Tips for Success
description: Learn about best practices and techniques for deploying, maintaining, and licensing of JAWS and ZoomText on an enterprise scale.

title: Rethinking Drag and Drop: Accessibility Insights
description: Discover the challenges of drag-and-drop widgets for assistive technology users. Learn when to use them, explore alternatives, and adopt best practices for accessible design. We'll share user feedback and insights to enhance drag-and-drop experiences.

title: Tactonom: Better Learning Opportunities for Blind People
description: A new technology to radically improve the learning opportunities of blind people will be presented. It seamlessly combines a huge free database of learning graphics with an auditory information stream to explain the content of each graphic.

title: TV Accessibility: Insights from BBC Research
description: Explore the BBC's research on accessibility in video-on-demand services, focusing on user sentiment, technological challenges, and the development of guidelines for improved media accessibility.

title: What's New with Chromebook Accessibility
description: Attendees will gain an understanding of the latest Chromebook assistive technologies. We will explore assistive technology such as enhanced low vision and magnification tools, Chromevox, live caption, dictation, and reading mode.

title: 200 Years of Global Innovation in Tactile Literacy
description: Become a tactile literacy champion by celebrating 200 years of Braille. This presentation will connect you with the tools, code and stories you can use to become a strong Braille advocate in your community.

title: Accessibility by Design: Strategies for Shifting Left
description: In this presentation we will explore how to shift left through inclusive designs, working backwards from customer delight and the strategies to enable development teams to build products that are accessible.

title: Accessibility Maturity Models
description: This talk will discuss accessibility maturity models and their importance on accessibility program success.

title: Accessible Communication Tools and Practices in the Workplace
description: The Digital Accessibility Office (DAO) will present the results our recent manual testing accessibility of Zoom, MS Teams, and Slack. We will discuss how the findings can be used to support individuals with disabilities in the workplace.

title: Accessible Multimodal Charts and Graphs on Touchscreens
description: Graphics dominate STEM education but remain largely inaccessible. We discuss graphical accessibility on touchscreens by comparing structured and free exploration for line and pie charts, and integrating spatial audio into scatter plots.

title: Achieving Compliance with PDF Documents
description: When it comes to PDF files, we want to make sure that our files are as accessible as possible. How do we do that? By making sure the files meet the standards that have been developed for the PDF format.

title: Designing Keyboard Behavior for Diverse User Types
description: Basic keyboard accessibility is not enough. Learn the varieties of different keyboard users and how to design and optimize keyboard interactions to meet their needs, including sighted keyboard users, screen reader users; basic and power users.

title: Ensuring AI Trust and Transparency with Inclusive Design
description: We must balance the value of AI branding with the customers' need to understand their risk of accepting Gen AI content, providing data, and understanding the AI process. Explore trauma-informed principles and inclusive design solutions.

title: Event Accessibility
description: What goes into making an event accessible? Learn what to consider when planning events, large or small. We will cover digital and in-person, from marketing and registration through day-of, logistics, and gathering feedback.

title: HRM Role in Leveraging Assistive Technology for Disabled Workers
description: This session explores how HR professionals can leverage AT to create inclusive workplaces for disabled employees. Attendees will learn practical HR strategies for integrating AT, improving accessibility, and enhancing job satisfaction and performance.

title: Increasing Assistive Technology Education
description: CSUN has a long and impressive history of involvement in many different aspects of disability. This session presents our programs that engage students in assistive technology, especially those who are traditionally underrepresented in STEM.

title: Making Building and Emergency Maps Accessible with Audiom
description: Learn how to transform inaccessible building and emergency PDF maps into fully WCAG AAA compliant experiences using Audiom, the inclusive map viewer and editor.

title: Real-Time Spatial Communication for the Deaf
description: Proto Hologram lets deaf individuals effortlessly engage by beaming life-size, real-time holograms. Discover how Proto enhances accessibility through real-time sign language interpretation, expressive 3D visual communication and remote access.

title: Testing Mobile Apps: Tools, Techniques and Best Practices
description: Evaluating iOS and Android apps for accessibility involves using tools like screen readers, alternate inputs, and automated scanners, along with other built-in OS settings. We'll dive into these tools.

title: The Human Side of Accessibility: Transforming UX with Data
description: This session advocates moving beyond basic a11y compliance by leveraging qualitative research. Attendees will learn how to uncover usability barriers, communicate accessibility impact through storytelling and codesign innovative solutions with users.

title: Wearable Digital Eyes Using Intelligent Stereo Vision
description: A compact navigation aide is built with stereo camera mounted under the visor of a cap. Using 3D robotic vision, the new algorithm enables a small wearable system to detect obstacles and generate descriptive voice alerts without Machine Learning.

title: A Comparison of Different Styles of Speech for Mathematics
description: Not happy with the way your screen reader reads math? There's more than one way to speak math. Come explore your options for how math is spoken by screen readers and learn how to "have it your way"...maybe.

title: Accessibility Evaluations for the Rest of Us
description: Learn about a free tool to help novices conduct step-by-step accessibility evaluations. Learn about our reusable, plain language accessibility resources and how to use ta11y to evaluate the maturity of your organization's accessibility practices.

title: Addressing the WebAIM Million Top Issues with Micro-Training
description: The WebAIM Million is an annual test of accessibility metrics that shows minimal progress. Come join our discussion about a new research-backed training resource to help practitioners address the WebAIM Million's top issues.

title: Bridging the Gap: Creating a Virtual AT Hub for Blind Veterans

presenters.name: Drew Metzger
presenters.affiliation: Augusta VA Medical Center - Blind Rehab Virtual Assistive Technology Hub

presenters.name: Victoria Wheeler
presenters.affiliation: Augusta VA Medical Center - Blind Rehab Virtual Assistive Technology Hub

description: Share the impact and outcomes of the Virtual Assistive Technology Hub, highlight best practices and strategies for effective virtual assistive technology delivery, discuss user feedback and lessons learned from hub operations and explore future directions.

title: Developing AI Equity, Access, and Inclusion for All
description: AI is everywhere, impacting disabled people in employment, healthcare, and beyond. The U.S. Access Board, alongside nonprofit and industry partners, has created best practices on disability and tech - if you want to know more, this session is for you!

title: Enhancing Digital Experiences for Cognitive Disabilities Using AI
description: In this session, participants will gain a deeper understanding of how AI can positively impact digital accessibility for users with cognitive disabilities.

title: Feeling a Thousand Words: Creating Tactile Graphics with Screen
description: Tactile graphics allow for an ongoing understanding of concepts or layouts of surroundings. This presentation seeks to empower those interested in the creation of tactile graphics through a simple yet intuitive program for those who are sighted, or those using screen readers like NVDA or JAWS.

title: ICT Design Implications for the Agency of BLV Individuals
description: A study of how ICT accessibility standards and related challenges uniquely limit the agency of BLV individuals relative to sighted individuals, found through a series of co-design workshops aimed at collaboratively designing ICT alternatives.

title: Keyboard Shortcuts for Faster, Mouseless Shopping on Amazon
description: Learn how Amazon's Worldwide Stores Accessibility team designed a new, keyboard-forward, feature that improves navigation for non-mouse users. Discover how we gained insights directly from customers with disabilities, and see for yourself how the new set of shortcuts streamline keyboard navigation and reduce friction while shopping.

title: Meeting ADA Compliance for Local Governments' Documents
description: The Department of Justice updated Title II of the ADA - implementing accessibility requirements for online content, including documents. We will share actionable tips for average government workers to create longstanding, accessible content.

title: Mobile App Accessibility: Guidelines, Cross-Platform Frameworks & More
description: This talk will provide a snapshot of the current mobile app accessibility landscape, focused on the state of mobile guidelines, how cross-platform frameworks impact the ecosystem, and the latest and greatest testing strategies.

title: Over, Around or Through: What Web Accessibility Taught Me
description: Johnny James looks back on almost 10 years in the industry, and what he's learned along the way, including, guess what, he had a disability all along and he didn't even know.

title: Prioritizing Accessibility in Education
description: Learn how to make accessibility the default in higher education. In this session, we will discuss ways to embed accessibility into organizational practices and brainstorm strategies to engage others and advocate for accessibility.

title: SceneryStack: A Web Stack for Accessible Interactives

presenters.name: Jesse Greenberg
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

presenters.name: Brett Fiedler
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

presenters.name: Taliesin Smith
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

description: Discover SceneryStack, the open-source web development stack and community powering accessibility for PhET Interactive Simulations. This session will explore its community-driven solutions with real-world applications and a roadmap for future growth.

title: SwiftUI Accessibility Techniques for Native iOS Apps
description: Learn how to build accessible iPhone and iPad apps using the CVS Health iOS SwiftUI Accessibility Techniques open-source project on GitHub and the iOS Accessibility Techniques app in the App Store.

title: Upgrade Your Training Program: A Data-Driven Approach
description: Explore the role of data in enhancing accessibility training. Discover how to use data to address accessibility issues, customize training programs, and continually refine accessibility initiatives through practical use-cases and best practices.

title: A Decade of Evolution: Crafting an Inclusive Accessibility Policy
description: A decade since UC's first accessibility policy. Join us to explore its evolution and current intricacies. We'll discuss how to integrate valuable lessons learned over the years into a new, more effective policy.

title: Accessible LMS Design for Blind and Low Vision Learners
description: This session details the design, implementation, and evaluation of an online learning platform resulting from co-designing sessions with K-12 teachers and blind and low vision (BLV) students.

title: Applying WCAG to Non-Web ICT
description: WCAG2ICT describes how the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) applies to non-Web documents and software. This session introduces the guidance added for WCAG 2.1 and 2.2, and explores how WCAG2ICT is used by other accessibility standards.

title: Creating a Hyper-Accessible Website for VI and Sighted Visitors
description: Gain an understanding of the many dimensions of web accessibility, learn techniques to identify hidden accessibility barriers and finding creative workarounds and gain key learnings and insights of what it takes to build a hyper-accessible site.

title: Design Guidelines of Online Supermarkets Apps for Older Adults
description: The authors report on the results of user testing for online supermarket apps already on the market, assuming the use of older adults. We also discuss a prototype of information design guidelines for online supermarket apps for older people.

title: Digital Accessibility Legal Update: EU
description: This session provides an overview of the European Accessibility Act and its implications for businesses that provide a range of digital products and services to consumers in the EU.

title: Explore Cloud-Based Live Streaming for Closed Captioning
description: Learn to enhance your organization's live streams with cloud-based closed captioning and AI-powered translation. This presentation covers best practices for accurate multilingual captions, the use of streaming protocols, and real-world examples.

title: FSCompanion: The Ultimate AI JAWS Assistant!
description: Explore how FSCompanion, an AI-powered assistant, helps users learn JAWS and Microsoft applications. This session demonstrates how FSCompanion provides concise, step-by-step instructions, improving productivity and accessibility for users.

title: Practical Strategies for Accessible Design
description: In this session, we will explore practical approaches and essential strategies for creating accessible digital experiences. As digital accessibility continues to be a key component of inclusive design, this session will provide actionable insights on how to implement accessibility principles without compromising creativity or user experience. Key topics include: Color Contrast & Design: Learn how to choose colors that ensure readability for users with visual impairments while meeting recognized accessibility standards. We’ll cover simple methods and tools that allow you to assess color contrast ratios effectively without diving deep into technical guidelines, making it easy to maintain both visual appeal and accessibility. Beyond Color: Discover how to use Figma to quickly assess color independence in your designs. We’ll walk through techniques for checking whether users can distinguish between different elements based on more than just color, ensuring accessibility for users with color vision deficiencies. Annotating Reading Order: Learn how to clearly communicate reading order to your developer, ensuring a logical flow for screen reader users. We’ll discuss practical methods for documenting and sharing information about the correct reading order using design tools, wireframes, or simple annotations. Descriptive Link Text: We’ll discuss best practices for creating meaningful and descriptive link text to provide context and clarity for users relying on screen readers or keyboard navigation. By the end of this webinar, you will have a toolkit of strategies and resources to enhance your designs, making them accessible to all users, regardless of ability. This session is perfect for designers, developers, and content creators looking to elevate their accessibility knowledge with practical, hands-on techniques. Join us to make your designs inclusive and accessible for everyone!

title: Redefining Blind Navigation with AI and Spatial Audio
description: Explore how music offers a superior solution for assistive navigation technology. Attendees will learn about the research behind music-based cues and why they provide a more intuitive, enjoyable experience than traditional speech-based solutions.

title: State of Interpreting Services for Blind and Low Vision
description: This session discusses how AI and human intelligence can enhance interpreting services for blind and low vision individuals, improving response times, personalization, and addressing key ethical and privacy concerns.

title: Teaching AI Apps to BVI Clients
description: Instructors from Braille Institute discuss teaching strategies for three AI apps; Seeing AI, Be My AI and Chat GPT. Which apps are right for different scenarios, the difference between blind and low vision usage, and in person vs. online teaching.

title: The Accessible Math Guide
description: In this presentation we will cover ways to create accessible math with various software tools. Participants will receive a copy of the NC VLC Accessible Math Guide.

title: Utilizing Assistive Technology Lab in Teacher Preparation
description: You will explore the AT lab virtually and learn how to set it up, including funding sources, item selection, and lab management. You will also learn how to utilize the lab in teacher preparation to improve AT planning, implementation, and assessment.

title: What Are You Wearing? Wearable Tech for Every Body!
description: This session will explore strategies to improve wearable accessibility, identify barriers for individuals with disabilities, and offer actionable solutions. Join us to discover the benefits and shape the future of wearable technologies.

title: Accessibility Consulting Is Broken
description: Traditional accessibility consulting is often little more than an audit. Using survey data from long term practitioners in the field, this take dives into the various other things that can be done to help customers improve and be self-sufficient.

title: AccessMap: Accessible Multimodal Routing and Wayfinding in Action

presenters.name: Kunal Mehta
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible Technology at the Paul G. Allen School

presenters.name: Anat Caspi
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible Technology at the Paul G. Allen School

presenters.name: Wisam Yasen
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible Technology at the Paul G. Allen School

description: AccessMap, Taskar's accessible planner, is designed to provide detailed way-finding for all users. This presentation covers the process of co-designing and refining with community the development of inclusive applications for multiple disabilities.

title: Achieving your Accessibility Goals with the ARC Platform
description: Learn how Program Managers and teams can leverage the ARC Platform, supported by ARC's KnowledgeBase trained AI Assistant, to achieve accessibility goals. Discover best practices for automated monitoring and long-term accessibility success.

title: Advancing Rural Access Together
description: Examine the challenges of distributing assistive technology and internet devices in rural areas. Join a collaborative session to develop solutions, enhance outreach, and build a network of providers dedicated to advancing equitable accessibility.

title: All Aboard! A Trip Through Europe's 7 Accessibility Standards
description: Hop aboard for an enlightening journey through the standards that power the European Accessibility Act.

title: Blind Innovators for the Blind: The Bill Gerrey Model
description: This session describes the hands-on approach taken by William Gerrey in developing and disseminating access technology for himself and his fellow blind employees, students and hobbyists. His techniques are a practical model for future projects.

title: CSUN's MS in Assistive Technology Studies and Human Services

presenters.name: S. Victoria Jaque
presenters.affiliation: CSUN MS in Assistive Technology Studies and Human Services

description: This presentation provides a description of CSUN's Master of Science in Assistive Technology Studies and Human Services, including admission requirements, support services, and professional opportunities.

title: Digital Accessibility Training in Higher Education
description: Learn how the Digital Accessibility Office (DAO) created and sustains a digital accessibility training program at the University of Colorado Boulder. This session includes training strategies, outreach, facilitation methods, and adaptable resources.

title: Evaluating Accessibility when Time Is Scarce
description: Let's explore accessibility evaluation more holistically and discuss how to use foundational questions, pre-evaluation planning and prioritizing, iteration, and free tools to make evaluation work more efficient and effective.

title: Mind the Gap: What Experts Forget When Teaching Accessibility
description: How can digital accessibility veterans train the next generation of practitioners? We'll go through some key teaching problems that experts run into, how to tackle them, and what areas of digital accessibility are prone to these traps.

title: Paige Connect: Bringing Technology to Low-Tech Braille

presenters.name: Jennifer Wenzel
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the Blind

presenters.name: Sarah Welch
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the Blind

description: Braille writers are a long standing and important part of education for braille learners. Paige Connect is a device that incorporates technology into these low-tech pillars of braille. Come explore one way that we can improve braille learning.

title: ParaCharts: Accessible Charts with Automatic Summaries
description: Making charts accessible is difficult for creators and organizations. Learn about ParaCharts, born-accessible charting software that uses multimodal interaction and automatic summaries to bring equivalent data experiences to people with disabilities.

title: Transforming LaTeX to HTML and EPUB Formats Using TEX4ht

presenters.name: Anita R. Walz
presenters.affiliation: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University

presenters.name: Sandeep Singh Sangha
presenters.affiliation: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University

description: LaTeX-authored Open Educational Resources (OER) often pose accessibility challenges for screen readers. This session will provide a compelling rationale for converting LaTeX source code into screen reader-friendly HTML and EPUB formats using TeX4ht.

title: Virtual Horizons: Transforming Mobility with VR/XR Technologies
description: Explore "Virtual Horizons," where we discuss innovative VR/XR tools that enhance mobility for individuals with physical disabilities. Discover resources, discuss challenges, and be inspired to champion accessibility in your community. Join us!

title: Accessibility Conformance Reporting of New Bluebook Features
description: The session highlights new accessibility features in Bluebook, College Board's proprietary application for delivering high-stakes SAT and AP exams. In that context, we will discuss our approach to authoring conformance and feature documentation.

title: Accessibility Considerations for Users with ADD and ADHD
description: Accessibility for individuals with cognitive disorders, such as Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), is a complex issue due to the wide range of disabilities encompassed by this term and the diverse needs of each person.

title: Accessible Videos: Going Beyond Captions
description: This session explores making video content accessible through captions, transcripts, audio descriptions, and visual design. Learn how AI can assist with these tasks, helping video editors, educators, and marketers create inclusive, user-friendly content.

title: Advancing Accessibility and Inclusion Through Open Source
description: This session will explore how Open Source Software and the broader open movement are improving accessibility and inclusion. It will also highlight opportunities for individuals and organizations across the accessibility ecosystem to contribute.

title: CANCELLED: Explore Apple Hearing Innovations: AirPods Pro and Beyond
description: Apple devices include a wealth of hearing accessibility features out of the box. Learn about Apple's approach to hearing accessibility, including demonstrations of hearing related features, and how to make the most of them.

title: Cutting Costs, not Corners: AI for Title II and EAA Compliance
description: Compliance with EAA and Title II of the ADA can be difficult due to budget constraints and diverse digital assets. This session explores leveraging AI within accessibility services to ensure a cost-effective and scalable path to compliance.

title: Demystifying Disability Access: Comprehensive ADA Compliance
