description: Achieving compliance with all the layered disability access laws and regulations can be about as clear as mud. This session lays out a framework of 10 steps any organization can take to maximize accessibility beyond just the digital realm.

title: Design Rebels: Rethinking the Principles of Design
description: Discover how to improve user experiences by challenging traditional design principles. Learn actionable strategies for inclusivity, enhance empathy, and apply new approaches across different design fields and roles.

title: Disclosure Accommodation and Interactional Ecosystems
description: The duty to accommodate process is the means by which we provide accessibility supports to students and employees with disabilities. Learn how this framework may fail the people it is intended to serve, and potential ways to improve its effectiveness.

title: Empower Change: Mobilize Your Community to Crowdsource Data

presenters.name: Kunal Mehta
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible Technology at the Paul G. Allen School

presenters.name: Anat Caspi
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible Technology at the Paul G. Allen School

description: Learn how to inspire and mobilize people to contribute to accessibility-first crowd-sourced transportation data. Discover the impact of collective efforts, engage with mobile apps, and gain practical strategies for creating inclusive communities.

title: Enhancing Spoken Interfaces with SSML and 'Data-SSML' Property
description: Explore the latest advancements in digital accessibility with Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML) and the 'data-ssml' property. Learn to control text-to-speech output for assistive technologies to ensure accurate, natural speech.

title: Making the Screen Reader Accessible by Voice
description: Discover how integrating voice interaction with screen readers creates a superior accessibility tool. This session explores the synergy between technologies, truly demonstrating that whole is greater than the sum of its parts.

title: Special Topics in STEM Accessibility: A Content Perspective
description: STEM content presents many accessibility puzzles: MathML, complex figures and tables, color-coded graphics, videos with information-dense visuals. This session explores basic best practices for solving these puzzles, with a focus on content creation.

title: Understanding Factors Affecting Tactile Graphics Complexity
description: This session presents an experimental investigation into how graphical complexity impacts tactile graphic identification for individuals with blindness and visual impairment.

title: Featured Presentation: A Tale of Two Washingtons: Partnering for Accessibility

presenters.name: Mary Craig
presenters.affiliation: Washington State Department of Services for the Blind

description: Although governed by different laws and policies, U.S. Federal and State Governments share accessibility goals. Learn how the U.S. Federal Government and the Washington State Government collaborate to advance digital accessibility from coast to coast.

title: A Scoping Review of AT for Autistic Individuals in Employment
description: ATs can benefit autistic individuals who want to pursue employment. This presentation reviews and synthesizes findings in relevant literature to inform the development and implementation of employment-focused ATs for this population.

title: Assistive Technology Management Using Microsoft Intune

presenters.name: Patrick Turnage
presenters.affiliation: Florida School for the Deaf and the Blind

description: With more people working and learning remotely, cloud management is changing how IT administrators manage devices. At the Florida School for the Deaf and the Blind we are using Microsoft Intune to also manage AT on Windows devices.

title: Be Audit Ready: Preparing for Manual Testing and Taking Action
description: Learn techniques to prepare for a successful manual accessibility audit, gain insight into the manual testing process, effectively interpret the results, and develop a successful remediation strategy that fits your needs.

title: Document Accessibility for Legal and Regulatory Compliance
description: This session will explore the evolving landscape of document accessibility compliance, focusing on international regulations and advanced monitoring strategies. Attendees will leave with practical tools to continuously adhere to legal requirements.

title: Evaluating an Accessible Science Tool for Students with Blindness
description: SRI Education and Alchemie partnered to develop and evaluate Kasi, an accessible, AI-enabled system to help blind or low vision (BLV) students learn chemistry. A single-case study was used to examine the effects of Kasi for BLV students.

title: Growing Your Career While Building a More Accessible Future
description: This session offers practical examples about how to keep your heart and mind engaged in accessibility (to avoid burnout) and how to raise accessibility awareness across your organization.

title: How to Incorporate Accessibility into Your Agile Sprints
description: Learn how to seamlessly integrate accessibility into your Agile sprints. Discover practical strategies to treat accessibility as technical debt, prioritize tasks by role, and maintain consistent progress, ensuring your digital products are inclusive.

title: Intersecting Cultural Competence and Accessibility for DEI
description: How do we ensure our accessibility work aligns with our commitments to diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging? Hear about the efforts at a university and a K-12 district to reduce both barriers and marginalization for all students.

title: Multimodal AI for Efficient English-to-Braille Transcription
description: Discover how multimodal AI agents can transform English-to-Braille transcription from taking days to minutes. We dive into our framework and how we hope it can be applied in the classroom to revolutionize how blind students learn and access content.

title: Navigating the Digital Divide: Enhancing Appliance Accessibility
description: Explore the importance of accessibility in home appliances and the challenges posed by inaccessible electronics. This talk highlights innovative solutions, user-friendly designs, and the significant impact on individuals with disabilities.

title: On-Demand ASL Interpreting as a Public Accommodation
description: This panel will discuss the impact of on-demand ASL interpreting services, focusing on how technology and innovation are transforming public accommodations for the Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing community.

title: Share Files and Collaborate in Teams with JAWS
description: Learn how to navigate, access, and share files in Microsoft Teams using the JAWS screen reader. This session covers key features for real-time file collaboration, managing settings, and accessing shared PowerPoint presentations during meetings.

title: The New Touchscreen Experience for Audiom Web Maps Is Here
description: Discover techniques for creating accessible touchscreen interfaces for non-semantic web apps like games, graphs, and maps. Learn how Audiom's new interface overcomes mobile screen reader and browser limitations.

title: Which Ebook Apps Have the A11y Features You Need in 2025?
description: DAISY Consortium is expert in accessible reading and has developed a robust protocol with print disabled people, newly updated for 2025. We'll review latest test results, highlighting the accessibility strengths and weaknesses of popular ebook apps.

title: A Braille User Interface for Multi-line Braille Displays
description: In this session we will present a new framework for multiline braille displays that reduces mental load and increases understanding through spatial structuring of information, context-rich tactile elements, and intuitive user interactions.

title: Accommodating Neurodiverse Users Using Chat Support
description: This presentation will focus on how chat support is often the preferred mode of support by autistic customers. Chat support provides the fast response of the telephone while removing barriers of speech, audio processing, and social expectations.

title: ADA Title II Compliance: Be Competitive & Compliant
description: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Title II basics, who is required to comply, and when, the implications of non-compliance, and key exceptions.

title: Coding Python with Screen Readers and a Touch of AI

presenters.name: Jeffrey Colon
presenters.affiliation: Lighthouse for the Blind and Visually Impaired (San Francisco)

presenters.name: Fernando Macias
presenters.affiliation: LightHouse for the Blind and Visually Impaired (San Francisco)

description: Join us for a beginner-friendly workshop that teaches Python coding using a screen reader. Participants will explore coding basics, learn how to navigate with assistive tech, and discover how AI can support their coding journey.

title: Deadline ADA: Get Your School Compliance-Ready for 2026
description: You CAN make the deadline! This session will focus on creating an actionable compliance roadmap that prioritizes tasks based on student impact. You will learn how to not only meet the due date - but to also truly improve educational access for all.

title: Enhance ACR/VPAT Authoring Efficiency with LLM-Based Automation
description: Learn how to streamline ACR/VPAT authoring with LLM-based automation. Explore the phased development from generating exceptions to full VPAT automation. Gain insights that enhance efficiency, accuracy, and cost-effectiveness across various fields.

title: Enhancing Accessibility in Datacenters and Industrial Spaces
description: Explore the Microsoft Global Datacenter Accessibility Program's efforts to enhance inclusive space design, secure sponsorship, address accessibility gaps, and foster an inclusive culture through training and diverse recruitment.

title: Evaluating Motivational Approaches in A11y Change Management
description: Different motivational energies can change accessibility culture. Incentive efforts such as role-specific training and recognition or enforcement efforts through accountability and requirements build sustainable improvement capabilities.

title: How GitHub Empowers Developers with Disabilities
description: Join the GitHub accessibility team for an overview of GitHub and the accessibility features that empower developers with disabilities to create, collaborate, and contribute.

title: How to Bring Your Best Self to Work
description: This session explores workplace modifications to support diverse work styles. Learn to identify work styles, understand disability disclosure, and implement informal adaptations for better performance.

title: Level Up: Redefining Adaptive Gaming on Chromebooks
description: Imagine playing your favorite mobile games using only facial expressions! Learn how Chromebook's new Face control technology was built and how gamers with mobility differences can play Android games using keyboards, switches, and facial expressions.

title: Novel Stimuli to Benchmark and Train Echolocation Skills
description: Echolocation aids blind individuals in navigating, but it is underutilized. We introduced optimized sounds to enhance spatial perception, improving echolocation accessibility. These stimuli could benefit training and be added to assistive devices.

title: Product Design for Neurodiversity
description: Explore strategies for designing neurodiverse-friendly digital products using WCAG guidelines. Discover new UX patterns and interactions tailored for neurodiversity, and learn practical approaches to reduce cognitive load and create inclusive, personalized user experiences.

title: Progressive Enhancement vs. Graceful Degradation: An Equity Issue
description: How do you deliver your service for the latest and older tech? Progressive Enhancement or Graceful Degradation? Which ensures equal access and outcomes? Which is more expensive for your organization and users?

title: Project Relate: Communication Made Easy for Non-Standard Speech
description: Explore the future of speech accessibility! Discover how machine learning enables personalized recognition and innovative features for diverse needs. A must for anyone interested in inclusive speech technology.

title: Teaching Accessibility to Young Coders
description: A lot of attention is being put on "shifting left" and inclusion of digital accessibility in higher education, but very little (or no) attention is being put on introducing digital accessibility to elementary school coders.

title: Accessible Cybersecurity
description: In this session, we'll learn the basics of cybersecurity, then dive deep with hands-on examples of how accessibility, security, and usability play out in your product - especially when building with cutting-edge technologies.

title: Accessible Events: Hosting Inclusive Meeting Experiences
description: Events intend to bring people together. Disabled people should be included as potential active participants. Event planners will be informed on how to incorporate accessibility planning during every stage of event planning.

title: Accessible Interactive Learning Tools for Chemistry
description: Creating accessible and inclusive digital learning tools for chemistry requires thoughtful design and development. We will share our work in creating these unique tools for a wide range of concepts from both high school and college chemistry.

title: Accessible Math Materials in Real-Time Using AI
description: BVI individuals have challenges accessing non-textual materials such as math and graphical representations in real-time. We demonstrate how AI systems can automate the process of conversion/generation of accessible materials in real-time.

title: Axe Innovations: AI in Next-Gen Accessibility Tools
description: The world of digital accessibility is on the cusp of a revolution, driven by rapid advancements in AI. In this talk, we'll show how AI is transforming Axe, the industry-leading accessibility tool. We'll unveil the latest of Deque's AI-powered accessibility tools.

title: Gamification for Raising Awareness of Color Universal Design
description: Gamification is a way to motivate and increase efficiency of learning. This session proposes a game to learn Color Universal Design (CUD) by experiencing how a protanope perceives colors. Also details experiment methods and effectiveness for university students.

title: Inaccessible Apps: What Can Be Done about Them?
description: Inaccessible apps are frustrating as they can be mediocre to bad, and they comprise the majority of a blind person's digital interaction. How do we deal with inaccessible apps and what can we do currently and in the future to improve them?

title: Inclusive Insights: Shaping the Future of Accessible Research
description: Learn to integrate accessibility and lived experiences into market research. With best practices and a screen reader demo, gain actionable steps to create inclusive research processes that enhance insights across various roles and industries.

title: Paper Playground: A Multimodal Approach to Interaction Design

presenters.name: Jesse Greenberg
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

presenters.name: Brett Fiedler
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

presenters.name: Taliesin Smith
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

description: Discover Paper Playground, an open-source tool that bridges paper prototyping with multimodal design. We will introduce the tool, highlight key features, and share examples of inclusive co-design with auditory displays and tangible interactions.

title: Pieces of an Accessibility Audit: What's in an Accessible Name?
description: One of the many things considered during an Accessibility Audit is checking the Accessible Name of various components. Have you heard the term "Accessible Name", or maybe been informed you need to provide (or adjust) an Accessible Name?

title: Sound Sense: UX Research for Inclusive and Accessible Sound Design
description: Sound can be a powerful tool for creating more inclusive, accessible experiences. This session will explore how sound impacts diverse users, including those with and without hearing impairments, and how environmental factors shape sound design in products.

title: The Accessibility of Mental Telehealth Services
description: Treating mental health conditions requires therapy and medication. With the proliferation of online providers, telehealth is the norm. We will address the patient experience under an accessibility lens and share findings from mainstream providers.

title: Virtual Perspectives: Enhancing Empathy and Addressing Bias with VR
description: Explore "Virtual Perspectives," where we reveal how VR/XR can uncover unconscious biases and build empathy. Experience simulations that allow you to step into others' shoes and discover tools for enhancing DEI initiatives in your organization. Join us!

title: What's New with Apple Accessibility
description: Learn how to use Apple's new built-in accessibility features including Hearing Aid on AirPods Pro 2 and Eye Tracking, and how to make the most of the latest innovations in AI and Machine Learning.

title: Accessibility in a Generative AI World
description: Discover how generative AI is reshaping the way content and code are produced and what this means for accessibility. Get actionable strategies to responsibly harness its power for accessible experiences.

title: Addressing Burnout and Resource Gaps in Digital Accessibility
description: We will address burnout in digital access, offering strategies to manage stress, advocate for resources, and sustain well-being. You will gain actionable insights to apply across industries for more effective, sustainable accessibility efforts.

title: Alexa How Do I Read Free Audio Books from NLS BARD?

presenters.name: Donald Olson
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

presenters.name: Zhi Huang
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

description: This introduces the My Talking Books skill, currently in open beta, to play free audio books from NLS BARD using the Amazon smart speaker. The presentation will cover requirements, provide a demonstration, and explain how to get help.

title: Artificial Intelligence Use and Perceptions by Disabled Adults
description: National study of disabled adults' use and perceptions of artificial intelligence (AI) use and perceptions surrounding AI.

title: Beyond Words: Navigating Audio Description for Diverse Audiences
description: Learn the audio description process and how the right technology and description partners can provide more engaging, accessible content that not only includes individuals of all backgrounds and abilities but also satisfies ADA Title II requirements.

title: CANCELLED: AI Might Solve Digital Accessibility, but Won't Fix Ableism
description: New tools and advancements in artificial intelligence will only get us so far in resolving issues with digital accessibility, at some point we need to address the root cause of ableism.

title: Cross-Disability Access to Kiosks: Lessons Learned
description: Self-service kiosks are widely used but often inaccessible to people with disabilities. This session will cover accessibility techniques that can be incorporated into kiosks, including newer, smaller and tabletop kiosks and tablets.

title: Digital Accessibility Legal Update: Beyond the U.S. and EU
description: Digital access is a human right of disabled people. This session shares legal updates about laws, regulations, and advocacy efforts about digital access in India, Africa, Canada and other countries outside the U.S. and EU.

title: Don't Make the Case, Make a Brand: Persuade like a Marketer
description: Are you finding it difficult to make the case for accessibility in your organization? Learn the techniques used by marketers to make your case persuasive and build momentum through communications campaigns that work.

title: Implementing a Comprehensive Course Accessibility Approach
description: In one-year, Northwestern reduced course site accessibility errors by 74%. This session offers a roadmap for replication, highlighting the multi-pronged approach of a collaborative campaign, tools, and tiered levels of support that created success.

title: Innovation in AT Tools: Improvements and Updates
description: Development and innovation is necessary in technology. This session will focus on tech topics important to higher ed and their development into AT tools. Topics such as data, AI, and learner centered adoption will be discussed and demonstrated.

title: Speakaboo: The Simplified Approach to a Complex Problem
description: This session will explore how effective design can undercut larger, established competitors and deliver intuitive, powerful solutions that don't sacrifice ease of use.

title: Accessibility of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Mental Health
description: This session will present initial findings from a comprehensive literature review investigating the accessibility of AI applications in improving mental health of individuals with cognitive and learning disabilities.

title: CANCELLED: An Instructional Designer's Approach to Accessibility Training
description: Instructional design focuses on the creation of engaging learning experiences. In this session, Wiley will discuss how we developed our digital accessibility training program, including engagement options, materials development, metrics, and more.

title: Embedding Accessibility into a Design System Playbook
description: How Design, Engineering, and Accessibility teams collaborated to integrate accessibility into a new design system and playbook at eBay, with practical insights and lessons learned.

title: Finding My Way: Options for Indoor Navigation by the Blind

presenters.name: Jule Ann Lieberman
presenters.affiliation: TechOWL, Institute on Disabilities at Temple University

description: This session explores indoor wayfinding for blind through various technologies and strategies for efficient navigation within new indoor settings.

title: Improving the Quality of Life with Accessibility, UD, and UI
description: As our population ages, more people will need features of digital accessibility, universal design, and inclusive design to conduct daily tasks easily. Learn how each of these add their own features to make the quality of life better for all.

title: Influence of Braille Display Design on Reading Performance
description: We explore the impact of line length and single vs. multi-line braille displays on adult reading performance, and the development of indicators to guide device selection. This theme will be of interest to designers, practitioners, and braille readers.

title: Lessons Learned from Applying AI as an Accessibility Assistant
description: Learn how ETS leverages AI large language and vision models to enhance productivity for our accessibility team. We'll explore AI tools for testing, content generation, and adaptation, while addressing privacy, bias, and inclusive evaluation.

title: NO SHOW: Implementing and Analyzing Procurement Accessibility at JPL
description: Join us to learn how NASA JPL has integrated accessibility into its procurement process. We'll share strategies for embedding accessibility and introduce an open-source tool designed to extract data from Accessibility Conformance Reports.

title: One Year In: Meeting the ADA Title II Timeline
description: Strategically planning for digital accessibility is critical and needs championing at the highest level. This session will provide ways to increase access, create buy-in, execute a sustainable access plan, find resources, and promote digital equity.

title: Shopify and WordPress: How to Make eCommerce Themes Accessible
description: Discover how to customize Shopify/WordPress themes for accessibility, ideal for business owners who are expanding market share or already serve diverse customers in eCommerce.

title: Taking Flight with the Monarch

presenters.name: Seth Leblond
presenters.affiliation: CATT - Center for Assistive Technology Training

description: Explore the Monarch, a 10-line refreshable braille display that integrates tactile graphics with braille. Learn how it speeds up access using Tactile Graphics and Bookshare, transforming tactile literacy for blind and low vision students.

title: The Usability Gap: Why Accessibility Does Not Always Mean Usability
description: This session examines the disparity between achieving compliance and creating products that are usable and delightful for individuals with disabilities. We explore barriers often observed in compliant products.

title: UDL for Physical, Academic, and Social Belonging
description: Explore how a sense of belonging and UDL principles can boost student engagement and success. This session covers optimizing physical environments, using diverse academic tools, and promoting social belonging for inclusive, engaging classrooms.

title: Achieving Outcomes Through Enabling Technology
description: Participants in this session learn how Keystone Human Services leveraged the integration of enabling technology, both high and low end, into life changing outcomes for people with intellectual disabilities.

title: AI-Powered Accessibility Auditing
description: This demonstration, and presentation, will explore the current capabilities of artificial intelligence in accessibility tools and the challenges of implementing them.

title: Building an A11Y Program to Meet Global Requirements
description: Accessibility requirements are expanding, and our budgets are not. Learn how Security and Privacy organizations have already solved many of our new challenges and real world examples of tools and processes you can leverage today.

title: Creating Accessible Design Systems
description: A hands-on workshop that guides participants in creating accessible design systems, including live reviews, remediation techniques, and documentation strategies.

title: Developing an Affordable Internet-Accessible Braille Display
description: Discover how the Bridge2Africa Braille Display empowers visually impaired students in South Africa by providing affordable, internet-accessible braille technology. This session covers its development, design challenges, and educational impact.

title: Enhancing Camera-Related Mobile Tasks for Blind Users
description: Mobile cameras are increasingly important tools for users with blindness, as demonstrated by our research on mobile check deposits. Drawing on these findings, attendees will learn design strategies to improve critical steps like preparation, framing, and confirmation in camera-based workflows.

title: HPE Innovation: Research-Driven Design for All Abilities
description: Equitable experiences and accessible technology are created through inclusive research and design. In this session, we will share our "Inclusive Lens" research framework and how it translated into actionable design changes in HPE GreenLake.

title: Rethinking Accessible Education: Accommodations to Anti-Ableism
description: In higher education and technical disciplines, educators, students and service providers contend with ongoing challenges to improve educational accessibility. Come learn how to understand and relate to the systemic barriers and ableist assumptions unpinning them.

title: Six Steps to a "Robust" Accessibility Program
description: Is your company aiming to meet Accessibility standards? Our presentation will show you how to centralize inventory, refine systems, identify accessible suppliers, and support non-engineering teams.

title: Strategies for Inclusive Chat UIs: A Usability Framework Approach
description: Join us to explore how text-based chat UIs are evolving in the Gen AI era, and how they can remain accessible to all users. We'll share a usability framework for evaluating chat patterns and discuss key principles for accessible & inclusive chatbots.

title: Top Down Browsing, a Modern Approach to Screen Readers
description: Screen readers do not do well interpreting the HTML, CSS and JavaScript. AI heuristics can be used to build a more natural interface to navigate and interact with existing web sites and is designed to work with most pages, ADA compliant or not.

title: Web-Based Auditory Description for Educational Interactives

presenters.name: Jesse Greenberg
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

presenters.name: Brett Fiedler
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

presenters.name: Taliesin Smith
presenters.affiliation: Department of Physics, University of Colorado Boulder, Boulder, CO

description: Explore how the Voicing Response System, a web-based auditory description tool, can enhance accessibility in educational simulations. Learn about its customizable features, inclusive design process, and application across educational simulations.

title: AI for Accessibility: Empowering Creators and Users
description: This session explores practical ways AI can help make digital content accessible for creators and users. Attendees will learn how to apply AI tools to real-world accessibility challenges, focusing on both content creation and consumption.

title: CANCELLED: Accessibility Champion Program: One Year Later What We Learned
description: Launching an Accessibility Champion Program can be daunting, especially with competing priorities. This session will discuss the logistics, curriculum and methodology we introduced in our first program and what we decided to take forward/leave behind.

title: Collaborating for a Common Cause: Accessible Health Care
description: Strong customer/vendor partnerships create a more accessible experience. You'll hear how a large integrated health organization and an electronic health care vendor enhanced patient care by going beyond the VPAT with trust, collaboration, and structure.

title: Driving Change for A11y with Six Sources of Influence
description: Building an effective accessibility center of excellence requires changes in important behaviors from everyone across the organization. This session explores the "Crucial Influence" model and how lessons learned can be applied to drive accessibility.

title: Free Braille and Audio Reading Equipment from NLS

presenters.name: Elizabeth Bottner
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

presenters.name: Zhi Huang
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

description: This session introduces and demonstrates the features of the NLS eReaders and the new Digital Talking Book player. Attendees will have an opportunity to have hands-on experience with the units and will learn how to obtain these units for themselves.

title: Gen AI Voice Tools for Enterprise Software Accessibility
description: Learn how Gen AI voice tools are being adapted by enterprise users to make their workflows more accessible and productive, as well as best practices for successfully (and accessibly) incorporating Gen AI voice tools into enterprise platforms.

title: How to Build an Accessibility Community
description: The organizers of the San Diego Accessibility & Inclusive Design group will walk you through what it takes to build an accessibility community. Learn how we obtain sponsors, book accessible venues and curate a diverse range of topics and speakers.

title: MABLESim: A Tool for Simulating Indoor Accessibility
description: MABLESim is a simulation framework for evaluating the accessibility of indoor environments. Users can interact with the simulation to explore how different mobility needs impact navigation, aiding in the design of more inclusive spaces.

title: The Intersection of WCAG and Computerized Testing for Kids
description: WCAG was not built with young children in mind. We explore ways that digital accessibility can be counterproductive for educational assessments and how we work to improve it. Join us to help accelerate learning for all students.

title: The Joy of 4 Legged AT: Puppy Raising Trainer and Recipient
description: Unique AT, service dogs, increase individuals with disabilities independence. Framed by service dog history and laws, this session covers benefits and aspects of the training of puppies to be service dogs and having a working service dog.

title: Universal Design for Learning Through Assistive Technology

presenters.name: Joy Mistovich
presenters.affiliation: The Butler Institute of American Art

description: This presentation will provide attendees with the foundational knowledge of Universal Design for Learning (UDL). Examples of high-tech and low-tech options will be discussed, along with key web design/development practices for UDL implementation.
